intro: Brandesia elongata, also known as Satureja, is a plant species belonging to the Lamiaceae family, commonly found in the Andean region of South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, providing relief from digestive issues, inflammation, and stress through its analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which contribute to its health benefits. Bioactive constituents of Brandesia elongata include essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, including carvacrol, thymol, and rosmarinic acid, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, Brandesia elongata is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and essential oils, which are commonly used to alleviate symptoms of gastrointestinal disorders, such as indigestion and bloating.
